summary: PR #2236 matched ifStatus_802F6194 in src/melee/if/ifstatus.c by removing a near-match inline helper traversal and rewriting the function as direct labeled control flow over HSD_GObj next_gx/next pointers. The author reported local objdiff and decomp.me verification, and the bot report recorded one new match from 99.81% to 100.00% for main/melee/if/ifstatus ifStatus_802F6194.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; removed nth_node helper and rewrote ifStatus_802F6194 traversal to reach a full match.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/if/ifstatus.c added 27 deleted 23 hunks 1
raw_diff.diff: diff for src/melee/if/ifstatus.c
human_pr_text.md: author says direct control-flow equivalent reaches full binary match

what_changed.text: Removed static inline HSD_GObj* nth_node(HSD_GObj* node, s32 n), which previously used a for loop helper to advance through next pointers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: deleted nth_node helper

what_changed.text: Removed the near-match comments on ifStatus_802F6194, including the 99.81% note and decomp.me scratch URL XGFpw.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: deleted comments before ifStatus_802F6194

what_changed.text: Rewrote ifStatus_802F6194 to initialize gx_head from node->next_gx, iterate with gx_cur and gx_next, and use labels check_done and advance_node rather than calling nth_node.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: added gx_head/gx_next/gx_cur/i locals and labeled control flow

what_changed.text: The bot report recorded ifStatus_802F6194 as one new match in main/melee/if/ifstatus, moving from 99.81% to 100.00%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01, 1 new match, ifStatus_802F6194 Before 99.81% After 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Kept the change tightly scoped to the target function and one translation unit instead of mixing in broader ifstatus cleanup.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: one changed file
raw_diff.diff: single hunk around ifStatus_802F6194

smart_moves.text: Preserved typed HSD_GObj pointer field access, using node->next_gx and gx_cur->next rather than raw pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: added node->next_gx and gx_cur->next accesses

smart_moves.text: Used explicit local names for traversal roles after removing the helper: gx_head, gx_next, and gx_cur.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: added HSD_GObj* gx_head, gx_next, gx_cur

smart_moves.text: Provided verification context in the PR body and received corroborating bot output for the target symbol.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: author claims local objdiff and decomp.me check
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows target reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a near-matched traversal helper, inlining the traversal as source-level direct control flow can sometimes recover the target binary shape when a clean helper abstraction does not match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: helper-based traversal replaced with direct control-flow equivalent
raw_diff.diff: nth_node call replaced by labeled traversal
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report changed ifStatus_802F6194 from 99.81% to 100.00%
caveats: This PR supports the tactic for this function only; it does not establish gotos as generally preferred over natural loops.
Author's local objdiff/decomp.me claim is supported by bot match output, but no detailed objdiff log is included in the slice.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a helper is only present to express a repeated pointer walk and the original binary appears to keep the walk in the function, removing the helper may be more matchable than preserving the abstraction.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: deleted nth_node and direct call
human_pr_text.md: near-match helper-based traversal was the thing replaced
caveats: Do not generalize this into avoiding helpers when nearby matched source or headers support a helper form.

naming_conventions.name: gx_head, gx_next, gx_cur
naming_conventions.text: The rewritten function uses short role-based local names for HSD_GObj traversal state.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: added HSD_GObj* gx_head, gx_next, gx_cur

naming_conventions.name: advance_node, check_done
naming_conventions.text: Labels describe the traversal step and loop-exit check in the direct control-flow rewrite.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: added advance_node and check_done labels

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace near-match helper call with direct labeled control flow.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The old version computed gx = node->next_gx and then called nth_node(gx, n). The new version keeps traversal state in local HSD_GObj pointers and branches between check_done and advance_node labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: old nth_node call versus new goto-based traversal
human_pr_text.md: direct control-flow equivalent intended to reach full binary match

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Keep explicit null checks during traversal.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The rewritten advance step assigns gx_next = NULL when gx_cur is NULL, otherwise advances to gx_cur->next, then returns gx_cur when done or when traversal terminates.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: added gx_cur NULL branch and final return gx_cur

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use bot-reported match delta as corroboration for a claimed match.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The decomp-dev bot reported one new match for ifStatus_802F6194 from 99.81% to 100.00%, backing the PR body's local objdiff/decomp.me claim.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: local objdiff and decomp.me claim

notes: No human review comments are present in the loaded slice, so review_feedback is empty.
The PR body claims local objdiff and decomp.me verification; the slice does not include raw objdiff output, but the decomp-dev bot report corroborates the final 100.00% match for the target symbol.
The direct goto-based shape is evidence-backed for this function, but should not be treated as a broad standard over natural loops without additional local evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Direct control-flow rewrite can close a helper traversal near-match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2236, ifStatus_802F6194 moved from a 99.81% near-match using an inline nth_node traversal helper to a 100.00% match by spelling the traversal directly with gx_head/gx_cur/gx_next locals and check_done/advance_node labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff
human_pr_text.md
text_corpus.jsonl

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify narrow exception for labeled direct control flow when verified
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note that although natural loops should be tried first, a direct labeled control-flow form may be acceptable when PR-local evidence shows it is needed to match a specific function and the change is tightly scoped. PR #2236 is one example: replacing a helper-based HSD_GObj traversal with direct labels was reported and bot-confirmed to take ifStatus_802F6194 from 99.81% to 100.00%.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: helper removed and labels added
text_corpus.jsonl: bot match report
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards prefer natural loops and caution against generated-looking control flow; this PR may be useful as a carefully evidenced exception candidate, not a general style rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that goto/labeled traversal is preferred style globally; the evidence only supports this one matched function.
Do not record reviewer intent or requested changes; no review comments were loaded.
Do not treat the deleted nth_node helper as known non-original beyond this PR's stated near-match context.
